steps.title: Put the equation in standard form
steps.content:
Expand the product and collect the terms on the left side:
$$x(x+11)-14=0$$
$$x^2+11x-14=0$$

steps.title: Apply the quadratic formula
steps.content: For $a=1$, $b=11$, and $c=-14$, calculate the discriminant: $$b^2-4ac=11^2-4(1)(-14)=121+56=177$$ Then substitute into the quadratic formula: $$x=\frac{-11\pm\sqrt{177}}{2}$$ The answer is D.
